title: Bank Statement Analysis for Auto Repair Shops
meta: Auto repair shops have parts-heavy revenue with high COGS. Learn how lenders analyze auto repair bank statements for business loans, MCA, and equipment financing.
content:
Auto Repair Shop Revenue Patterns

Auto repair shops are among the most consistently funded small businesses in the MCA and business loan market. They have steady, recurring customer relationships, essential services that aren't easily replaced, and relatively predictable revenue streams tied to vehicle maintenance cycles.

How Auto Repair Revenue Flows Through Bank Accounts

  • Card processing settlements: Daily deposits from repair invoices paid by card
  • Cash and check deposits: Regular cash and check payments, often bundled
  • Insurance company payments: For collision repair shops, large periodic payments from auto insurers
  • Fleet account receivables: Monthly billing for fleets means larger, scheduled deposits

High Parts Costs as a Complication

Auto repair has high cost of goods — parts can represent 40–60% of revenue. Large outflows to NAPA, O'Reilly Auto Parts, AutoZone, or dealer parts departments are normal business expenses. Lenders who flag these large outflows as red flags don't understand the business model.

AI bank statement analysis tools like StatementScrub calculate net cash flow (deposits minus withdrawals) alongside gross deposits — which gives a more accurate picture of a high-COGS business like auto repair.

Key Metrics for Auto Repair Lenders

  • Average daily card settlement deposits (activity indicator)
  • NSF history — should be minimal given regular daily deposits
  • Parts account payment consistency (showing normal business operations)
  • Equipment loan payments (lifts, diagnostic equipment, alignment machines)
  • Absence of MCA stacking

Common Loan Products

  • Equipment financing: For lifts, alignment equipment, diagnostic tools
  • Working capital loans / MCA: For inventory restocking or slow periods
  • Real estate loans: Auto repair shops frequently own their buildings
  • SBA loans: For acquisition or major expansion

Related: MCA underwriting checklist | Small business cash flow red flags

Bottom Line

Auto repair shops are strong candidates for business lending. Their essential service nature, recurring customer base, and consistent daily deposits make bank statement analysis straightforward. The main nuance is accounting for high parts COGS when evaluating net profitability from gross deposits.
